response.justification: The candidate meets baseline experience requirements (19 years engineering, Bachelor's CS) but lacks critical qualifications for a Principal Engineer — AI Strategy role. Current AI-agent infrastructure work is recent (April 2025, ~2 months) and involves code generation and frameworks rather than strategic product/platform development. No Master's/PhD in AI, no demonstrated AI product track record, no evidence of principal-level strategic leadership, and no experience advising customers on AI adoption—all preferred or key-responsibility elements. This is a strong mid-level full-stack engineer without the specialized AI expertise or principal-level strategic influence the role requires.
strengths: 19 years of consistent engineering across web, mobile, desktop, and infrastructure with demonstrated software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, microservices)
Recently engaged with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, showing awareness of current AI tooling landscape
Founder of open-source GIS platform with knowledge-sharing through workshops, demonstrating entrepreneurial thinking and mentorship capability
concerns: AI experience is extremely recent (2 months) and limited to infrastructure/code generation, not strategic AI product or platform development
No Master's/PhD in AI-related field; technical background is full-stack web and infrastructure development, lacking specialized ML/AI domain expertise

Career trajectory shows Senior-level positions without evidence of Principal or Director-level individual contributor impact, strategic architectural leadership, or advising customers on technology adoption

key_factors.factor: Missing demonstrated AI product/platform expertise and strategic AI track record
Career level is Senior IC, not Principal/Director IC with strategic influence
Solid foundational engineering across multiple domains and technologies
